input.command:
python3 -c "
import json
# Washington Post layoffs - check for publisher change
with open('articles/2026/02/05/articles.jsonl') as f:
    for line in f:
        a = json.loads(line)
        title = (a.get('title','') or '').lower()
        if 'washington post' in title:
            print(a.get('title','')[:160])
            content = a.get('content','') or ''
            # Look for publisher mention
            for kw in ['publisher', 'will lewis', 'lewis', 'replace', 'new leader']:
                idx = content.lower().find(kw)
                if idx >= 0:
                    print(f'  [{kw}]: ...{content[max(0,idx-100):idx+200]}...')
            print('---')
" | head -20

input.command:
python3 -c "
import json
# Will Lewis still publisher in Feb 2024 articles. Check if he's stepped aside
with open('articles/2026/02/05/articles.jsonl') as f:
    for line in f:
        a = json.loads(line)
        content = (a.get('content','') or '').lower()
        if 'washington post' in content and ('step' in content or 'resign' in content or 'depart' in content or 'new publisher' in content):
            title = a.get('title','')[:160]
            if 'washington post' in title.lower():
                for kw in ['step', 'resign', 'depart', 'new publisher', 'replac']:
                    idx = content.find(kw)
                    if idx >= 0 and 'lewis' in content[max(0,idx-100):idx+200]:
                        print(f'{title}')
                        print(f'  [{kw}]: ...{content[max(0,idx-100):idx+200]}...')
                        break
                print('---')
" | head -15
